assertion_results.text: The answer mentions `pytest --cov`, `pytest-cov`, or an equivalent pytest coverage command or plugin
assertion_results.evidence: The grader did not return a result for this check.

assertion_results.text: The answer recommends prioritizing meaningful, risky, or critical paths rather than blindly chasing one global percentage target
assertion_results.evidence:
"Don’t chase a single percentage everywhere—use coverage as a guardrail, not the goal."
"Enforce higher coverage on changed/critical code instead of the whole repo."
"Target behavior and risk: - Prioritize public APIs, money/availability paths, bug-prone and complex modules..."

assertion_results.text: The answer does not treat raw coverage percentage as the only quality signal
assertion_results.evidence:
"Don’t chase a single percentage everywhere—use coverage as a guardrail, not the goal."
"Improve signal, not just percentage: - Use branch coverage... - ...property-based (hypothesis) tests... - Consider mutation testing (mutmut)..."
Also suggests diff-based gating: "diff-cover ... --fail-under=90"
